svg.text: FRCS Part 1 (General Surgery) · colonoscopy · the diagnostic chain: shape → surface → depth → risk → management
Protruded (I) if >2.5 mm tall (closed-forceps height). Depressed / excavated (IIc, III) carry the highest risk of submucosal invasion.
Optical diagnosis — surface pattern predicts histology / invasion
I normal · II hyperplastic (stellar) · IIIS small round (depressed)
1 hyperplastic / serrated · 2 adenoma · 3 deep submucosal cancer
1 HP / SSL · 2A low-grade adenoma · 2B high-grade / superficial sm
NBI algorithm to identify sessile serrated lesions (SSLs)
Kudo Vn / NICE 3 / JNET 3 predict deep invasion — do not attempt endoscopic resection; refer for staging.
Depth of submucosal invasion — the key predictor of nodal spread
Sessile lesions are Haggitt 4 by definition (no stalk). Pedunculated tolerates depth to ~3000 µm.
Endoscopic specimens lack musc. propria → use absolute depth:
<1000 µm = low risk · >1000 µm = high risk
LOW RISK — local excision may suffice
Tumour budding (ITBCC 2016): Bd1 0–4 · Bd2 5–9 · Bd3 ≥10 buds per 0.785 mm². LVI & high budding independently predict nodal disease.
Low-risk: endoscopic resection (EMR / ESD) or local excision — rectum: TEM / TAMIS — then surveillance.
High-risk: segmental colectomy / TME with lymphadenectomy. All decisions via MDT — UK: ACPGBI SPECC pathway.
SSL: right-sided, flat, easily missed — BRAF /
CIMP / MLH1 methylation → serrated route to CRC.
(1) ≥5 serrated proximal to rectum, all ≥5 mm,
(2) >20 serrated of any size, ≥5 proximal to rectum.
Cumulative lifetime count. Commonest polyposis syndrome.
Manage by endoscopic clearance of all polyps;
Kikuchi LNM risk & 1000 µm threshold: Kitajima / Kikuchi; European (ESGE) guidance. Serrated polyposis: WHO 2019. Budding: ITBCC 2016. pT1 management: UK ACPGBI SPECC / MDT.
Revision aid — verify against current guidance before clinical use. (Spigelman duodenal staging reserved for UGI poster.)
Revision resources & realistic mock exams · mockfrcs.co.uk
